summary: Added the typed SerpProvider contract, deterministic MockSerpProvider, provider factory exports, focused tests, npm test script, and README documentation for the mock provider slice.

tests_run.result: Passed: Next.js production build completed. Build emitted @next/swc-win32-x64-msvc invalid Win32 application warnings but exited successfully.

decisions: Used node:test via tsx as the smallest TypeScript-friendly test setup.
Only provider name supported by the factory is "mock"; unsupported names throw immediately.
Mock limits default to 10, truncate fractional values, clamp below 0 to 0, and clamp above 50 to 50.
risks: npm install reported 2 moderate audit findings; remediation was left out of scope.
Next build reports SWC Windows binary warnings despite successful exit.
requested_action: Review the provider boundary, mock determinism contract, and factory behavior for acceptance.
